reference_answer: Summary of NLRB Decisions for Week of April 20 - 24, 2026, published by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB).

Chairman Murphy and Members Prouty and Mayer.

Section 8(a)(3) and (1) — discriminating in regard to the hire or tenure or terms and conditions of employment, thereby discouraging membership in a labor organization; and Section 8(a)(5) and (1) — failing and refusing to bargain collectively with the exclusive collective-bargaining representative of its employees.
Section 2(3) of the National Labor Relations Act governs the exclusion of agricultural laborers, and the Board uses the Fair Labor Standards Act definition in determining that exclusion.
The Ninth Circuit (U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit) enforced the Board's order in an unpublished opinion that issued on Tuesday, April 21, 2026.
Collins v. Yellen, 594 U.S. 220 (2021).

Because the General Counsel failed to prove effective service to Respondent SAAS Hotels NJ LLC, d/b/a La Quinta Inn & Suites Fairfield.

Member Mayer concurred and stated that the Board should continue its case-by-case approach in distinguishing between excluded agricultural laborers and included statutory employees, especially in "emerging or rapidly industries."

Case numbers 09-CA-366492 and 09-CA-367418; Dublin, OH. The April 20, 2026 decision denied the Respondent's request for Special Permission to Appeal the informal rulings purportedly made by the judge during a pre-hearing conference call which granted in part the General Counsel's and the Union's petitions to revoke the Respondent's subpoena duces tecum. The April 21, 2026 decision denied the Respondent's request for Special Permission to Appeal the informal rulings purportedly made by the Administrative Law Judge during a pre-hearing conference call which denied in part the Respondent's petition to revoke the General Counsel's subpoena duces tecum; the April 21 decision also denied the Respondent's request to stay the hearing and its motion to stay its subpoena and privilege log. Charges in both were filed by the International Brotherhood of Teamsters (IBT), Local Union No. 284.
